subject: Science
description: compare and contrast prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, including their complexity, and compare and contrast scientific explanations for cellular complexity

subject: Science
description: investigate and explain cellular processes, including homeostasis and transport of molecules

subject: Science
description: compare the structures of viruses to cells, describe viral reproduction, and describe the role of viruses in causing diseases such as human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and influenza

subject: Science
description: describe the stages of the cell cycle, including deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) replication and mitosis, and the importance of the cell cycle to the growth of organisms

subject: Science
description: examine the process of meiosis and describe how this process can result in genetic variation

subject: Science
description: describe the roles of DNA, ribonucleic acid (RNA), and environmental factors in cell differentiation

subject: Science
description: explain the importance of the cell in the hierarchy of biological organization, from molecules to ecosystems

subject: Science
description: recognize that components that make up the cell membrane provide critical structural and functional roles

subject: Science
description: identify components of DNA, and describe how information for specifying the traits of an organism is carried in the DNA

subject: Science
description: explain the purpose and process of transcription and translation using models of DNA and RNA

subject: Science
description: identify and illustrate changes in DNA and evaluate the significance of these changes

subject: Science
description: recognize that gene expression is a regulated process and identify the mechanisms by which gene expression may be altered

subject: Science
description: define and use the terms dominant, recessive, codominant, sex-linked, polygenic, phenotype, genotype, homozygous, heterozygous, and Punnett square

subject: Science
description: predict possible outcomes of various genetic combinations such as monohybrid crosses, dihybrid crosses, and incomplete dominance

subject: Science
description: recognize the significance of meiosis to sexual reproduction and describe the process of fertilization

subject: Science
description: analyze and evaluate how natural selection produces change in populations, not individuals

subject: Science
description: analyze and evaluate scientific explanations concerning any data of sudden appearance, stasis, and sequential nature of groups in the fossil record

subject: Science
description: analyze and evaluate how the elements of natural selection, including inherited variation, the potential of a population to produce more offspring than can survive, and a finite supply of environmental resources, result in differential reproductive success

subject: Science
description: analyze and evaluate the relationship of natural selection to adaptation and to the development of diversity in and among species

subject: Science
description: describe the interactions that occur among systems that perform the functions of transport, reproduction, and response in plants

subject: Science
description: describe the interactions that occur among systems that perform the functions of regulation, nutrient absorption, reproduction, and defense from injury or illness in animals

subject: Science
description: describe the role of ecological succession in ecosystems

subject: Science
description: investigate and analyze how organisms, populations, and communities respond to external factors

subject: Science
description: summarize the role of microorganisms in both maintaining and disrupting the health of both organisms and ecosystems

subject: Science
description: interpret relationships, including predation, parasitism, commensalism, mutualism, and competition, among organisms

subject: Science
description: compare variations and adaptations of organisms in different ecosystems
